title: Dashboard charts sort by x-axis not working
excerpt: My dashboards are typically shown as bar charts sorted by “x-axis ascending”, where the x-axis is “owner”. I just noticed that it no longer filters alphabetically (first or last name). This seems to be a bug with recent …

posts.author: tiger (Jay)
posts.cookedHtml: My dashboards are typically shown as bar charts sorted by “x-axis ascending”, where the x-axis is “owner”. I just noticed that it no longer filters alphabetically (first or last name). This seems to be a bug with recent updates but I could be wrong. Are others experiencing the same issue?

posts.author: bradley (Bradley Vanderhorst)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @tiger - did you reach out to support@monday.com so we can isolate this issue and escalate to the dev team?

posts.author: kerstyn (Kerstyn)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m seeing the same thing. Any news on how to fix it?

posts.author: TRB-monday.com (Talia)
posts.cookedHtml: Hey @kerstyn, would you be able to reach out to support@monday.com about this? I ask because our dashboard charts have had several improvements and changes since January so the underlying issue is likely different.

posts.author: kerstyn (Kerstyn)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks for replying. The support team has responded very quickly.

posts.author: SHardham (Simon Hardham)
posts.cookedHtml:
I am having the same issue. If I sort by descending it gives the reverse order of what I want.
However if I sort by ascending the order is wrong.
The issue is around status 0 and status 1 is in the wrong order.

posts.author: system (system)
posts.cookedHtml: This topic was automatically closed 7 days after the last reply. New replies are no longer allowed.

posts.author: jennap (Jenna)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @SHardham

For situations like this, we would ask that you reach out to our Support Team at support@monday.com so that we can dig into any unexpected behavior accordingly. Our Bug Buster team would be more than happy to troubleshoot with you and escalate as needed

Cheers,
Jenna
